clusters.title: Rescue teams continue to find survivors of Venezuela earthquakes
clusters.neutral_headline: Venezuela rescuers race against the clock to find more survivors
bullet_summary: The wife and two children of Argentine soccer star Lucas Trejo died after powerful earthquakes struck Venezuela, his team said
Hopes were fading Monday that survivors might still be found from the powerful twin earthquakes that rocked Venezuela, even as more international teams arrived to boost desperate search efforts

items.title: Rescue teams continue to find survivors of Venezuela earthquakes
items.standfirst: Tens of thousands of people are still presumed to be missing after two powerful earthquakes struck Venezuela last week.
items.content: Tens of thousands of people are still presumed to be missing after two powerful earthquakes struck Venezuela last week.

items.title: Thousands of Venezuelans still missing after earthquakes as search for survivors continues
items.standfirst: Tens of thousands of people remain missing after two powerful earthquakes rocked Venezuela last week. There is now a global effort to find survivors, including crews from the U.S. Camilo Montoya-Galvez reports.
items.content: Tens of thousands of people remain missing after two powerful earthquakes rocked Venezuela last week. There is now a global effort to find survivors, including crews from the U.S. Camilo Montoya-Galvez reports.

items.title: Soccer star's wife and 2 children killed in Venezuela earthquakes, team says
items.standfirst: The wife and two children of Argentine soccer star Lucas Trejo died after powerful earthquakes struck Venezuela, his team said.
items.content: The wife and two children of Argentine soccer star Lucas Trejo died after powerful earthquakes struck Venezuela, his team said.

items.title: Rescue window closing in Venezuela as quake death toll mounts
items.standfirst: Emergency teams with rescue dogs were searching Sunday for any remaining survivors of powerful twin earthquakes that struck Venezuela, where the death toll has surpassed 1,450 and the critical 72-hour window for rescuing trapped victims following a natural disaster has now passed.
items.content: Emergency teams with rescue dogs were searching Sunday for any remaining survivors of powerful twin earthquakes that struck Venezuela, where the death toll has surpassed 1,450 and the critical 72-hour window for rescuing trapped victims following a natural disaster has now passed.

items.title: Venezuela earthquakes: Father and son rescued as international teams help search
items.standfirst: A ​father and his son were pulled out alive from the rubble of a ​collapsed building on Sunday, four days after the devastating earthquakes that struck Venezuela. It was a scene that gavehope to the French and U.S. rescue workers active in the ​area as ‌they race against the clock to find more survivors.
items.content: A ​father and his son were pulled out alive from the rubble of a ​collapsed building on Sunday, four days after the devastating earthquakes that struck Venezuela. It was a scene that gavehope to the French and U.S. rescue workers active in the ​area as ‌they race against the clock to find more survivors.

items.title: Anger and grief as hopes fade of finding Venezuela earthquake survivors
items.standfirst: Hopes were fading Monday of finding survivors more than four days after powerful twin earthquakes struck Venezuela, as residents grow increasingly frustrated with the government's response to the disaster that has killed at least 1,450 people.
items.content: Hopes were fading Monday of finding survivors more than four days after powerful twin earthquakes struck Venezuela, as residents grow increasingly frustrated with the government's response to the disaster that has killed at least 1,450 people.

items.title: Venezuela: Mother and baby among earthquake survivors as rescue time runs out
items.standfirst: Over 1,400 people have died following twin #earthquakes in #Venezuela on June 24. #Rescue teams are still looking for survivors, although hope is fading. The 72-hour window after natural #disasters is the key moment to find the living, according to experts.
items.content: Over 1,400 people have died following twin #earthquakes in #Venezuela on June 24. #Rescue teams are still looking for survivors, although hope is fading. The 72-hour window after natural #disasters is the key moment to find the living, according to experts.

items.content: Hopes were fading Monday that survivors might still be found from the powerful twin earthquakes that rocked Venezuela, even as more international teams arrived to boost desperate search efforts

items.content: Venezuela rescuers race against the clock to find more survivors.

items.title: Mum rescued from Venezuela rubble with newborn baby tells BBC how he helped her survive
items.standfirst: The "miracle" rescue of 18-day-old baby Juan David is a symbol of hope in Venezuela after the devastating earthquakes.
items.content: The "miracle" rescue of 18-day-old baby Juan David is a symbol of hope in Venezuela after the devastating earthquakes.

items.title: Venezuela earthquake: father and son found alive in rubble after four days as death toll nears 1,500
items.standfirst: President says ‘we always hold onto hope’ as discovery of earthquake survivors spurs fresh search efforts despite dwindling chances of survivalMortuary in Caracas ‘overwhelmed’ as Venezuela struggles to respond after earthquakesA man and his teenage son were found alive under the rubble in Venezuela on Sunday, in a town about 40km north of the capital Caracas, AFP journalists reported, as the death toll from last week’s twin earthquakes passed 1,450.The discovery of survivors in Caraballeda was made by French and American rescue teams nearly four days after back-to-back quakes of magnitude 7.2 and 7.5 struck, completely destroying nearly 200 buildings in the area. Continue reading...
items.content: President says ‘we always hold onto hope’ as discovery of earthquake survivors spurs fresh search efforts despite dwindling chances of survivalMortuary in Caracas ‘overwhelmed’ as Venezuela struggles to respond after earthquakesA man and his teenage son were found alive under the rubble in Venezuela on Sunday, in a town about 40km north of the capital Caracas, AFP journalists reported, as the death toll from last week’s twin earthquakes passed 1,450.The discovery of survivors in Caraballeda was made by French and American rescue teams nearly four days after back-to-back quakes of magnitude 7.2 and 7.5 struck, completely destroying nearly 200 buildings in the area. Continue reading...

items.title: Another earthquake felt in Venezuela as families search for missing loved ones
items.standfirst: Another earthquake shook Venezuela today, as families continue searching for missing loved ones three days after twin quakes rocked that country. Ana Vanessa Herrero reports from Caracas for NBC News.
items.content: Another earthquake shook Venezuela today, as families continue searching for missing loved ones three days after twin quakes rocked that country. Ana Vanessa Herrero reports from Caracas for NBC News.

items.title: Venezuela earthquake death toll rises above 1,400
items.standfirst: The increasingly desperate search for survivors i n Venezuela entered a third day Saturday as people dug through the rubble of collapsed homes and apartment buildings after the devastating one-two punch of 7.2 and 7.5 magnitude earthquakes, knowing time is running out.
items.content: The increasingly desperate search for survivors i n Venezuela entered a third day Saturday as people dug through the rubble of collapsed homes and apartment buildings after the devastating one-two punch of 7.2 and 7.5 magnitude earthquakes, knowing time is running out.

clusters.title: Supreme Court says states can count mail ballots that arrive after Election Day
clusters.neutral_headline: Supreme Court blocks Trump's attempt to fire Federal Reserve governor Lisa Cook
bullet_summary: In its opinion, the court said that Trump does not have the constitutional authority to fire a Fed governor without cause
Reported by CBS News, Guardian World, BBC World and others

items.title: Supreme Court says states can count mail ballots that arrive after Election Day
items.standfirst: The Supreme Court on Monday ruled that states can count mail ballots that are cast by Election Day but arrive later, rejecting a GOP challenge to a Mississippi law.
items.content: The Supreme Court on Monday ruled that states can count mail ballots that are cast by Election Day but arrive later, rejecting a GOP challenge to a Mississippi law.

items.title: Supreme court goes against Trump in rulings on Fed firing of Lisa Cook, E Jean Carroll appeal and mail-in ballots – US politics live
items.standfirst: Court does rule that Trump can fire leaders of independent agencies but says firing of Cook was unlawful, declines to hear appeal on Carroll case and upholds law to count mail-in ballots arriving after election dayUS supreme court rejects Trump’s bid to appeal $5m E Jean Carroll verdictUS supreme court upholds law to count mail-in ballots arriving after election dayUS supreme court rules Trump’s firing of Lisa Cook from Fed was unconstitutionalSign up for the Breaking News US emailThe supreme court is due to release some of its final opinions at 10am ET, with major decisions including on Donald Trump’s effort to end birthright citizenship and to fire Federal Reserve governor Lisa Cook still to come.Last week the court handed the Trump administration huge wins in major rulings on immigration. It gave the administration a green light to block asylum seekers at the US-Mexico border, in a decision that fundamentally reshapes the US asylum system. And it also ruled in favor of the administration’s bid to strip temporary protected status from hundreds of thousands of Haitians and Syrians, who were legally in the US and protected from deportation. The decisions, powered by court’s conservative justices, saw the supreme court accused of advancing a white supremacist agenda. Continue reading...
items.content: Court does rule that Trump can fire leaders of independent agencies but says firing of Cook was unlawful, declines to hear appeal on Carroll case and upholds law to count mail-in ballots arriving after election dayUS supreme court rejects Trump’s bid to appeal $5m E Jean Carroll verdictUS supreme court upholds law to count mail-in ballots arriving after election dayUS supreme court rules Trump’s firing of Lisa Cook from Fed was unconstitutionalSign up for the Breaking News US emailThe supreme court is due to release some of its final opinions at 10am ET, with major decisions including on Donald Trump’s effort to end birthright citizenship and to fire Federal Reserve governor Lisa Cook still to come.Last week the court handed the Trump administration huge wins in major rulings on immigration. It gave the administration a green light to block asylum seekers at the US-Mexico border, in a decision that fundamentally reshapes the US asylum system. And it also ruled in favor of the administration’s bid to strip temporary protected status from hundreds of thousands of Haitians and Syrians, who were legally in the US and protected from deportation. The decisions, powered by court’s conservative justices, saw the supreme court accused of advancing a white supremacist agenda. Continue reading...

items.title: Supreme Court blocks Trump's attempt to fire Federal Reserve governor Lisa Cook
items.standfirst: The decision, seen as a win for central bank independence, sends the fight over removal back to the lower courts.
items.content: The decision, seen as a win for central bank independence, sends the fight over removal back to the lower courts.

items.title: Supreme Court rejects Trump bid to fire Fed's Lisa Cook as legal case continues
items.standfirst: The Supreme Court allowed Lisa Cook to continue in her post as a member of the Federal Reserve Board of Governors while legal proceedings over President Trump's attempt to fire her continue.
items.content: The Supreme Court allowed Lisa Cook to continue in her post as a member of the Federal Reserve Board of Governors while legal proceedings over President Trump's attempt to fire her continue.

items.title: Supreme Court blocks Trump from sacking Fed governor Lisa Cook
items.standfirst: Justices fail to over-rule the president from firing a regulator in a separate decision
items.content: Justices fail to over-rule the president from firing a regulator in a separate decision

items.title: US supreme court rules Trump can fire leaders of independent agencies
items.standfirst: Case focused on White House’s March 2025 firing of Federal Trade Commission member Rebecca SlaughterUS supreme court decisions – live updatesSign up for the Breaking News US emailThe US supreme court ruled that Donald Trump can fire leaders of independent agencies or commissions, ending 90 years of court precedent that curbs executive power.The vote in the case of Trump v Slaughter is 6-3, with dissents from Justices Sonia Sotomayor, Ketanji Brown Jackson and Elena Kagan. Continue reading...
items.content: Case focused on White House’s March 2025 firing of Federal Trade Commission member Rebecca SlaughterUS supreme court decisions – live updatesSign up for the Breaking News US emailThe US supreme court ruled that Donald Trump can fire leaders of independent agencies or commissions, ending 90 years of court precedent that curbs executive power.The vote in the case of Trump v Slaughter is 6-3, with dissents from Justices Sonia Sotomayor, Ketanji Brown Jackson and Elena Kagan. Continue reading...

items.title: US supreme court rules geofence warrants require constitutional privacy protections
items.standfirst: Law enforcement’s use of warrants sweeping smartphone location data requires privacy protections, court rulesUS supreme court decisions – live updatesThe US supreme court has ruled that law enforcement’s use of sprawling warrants that sweep up smartphone location data requires privacy protections under the fourth amendment, in a boost to critics who view their use as an unconstitutional dragnet.Justice Elena Kagan wrote the majority opinion. Continue reading...
items.content: Law enforcement’s use of warrants sweeping smartphone location data requires privacy protections, court rulesUS supreme court decisions – live updatesThe US supreme court has ruled that law enforcement’s use of sprawling warrants that sweep up smartphone location data requires privacy protections under the fourth amendment, in a boost to critics who view their use as an unconstitutional dragnet.Justice Elena Kagan wrote the majority opinion. Continue reading...

items.title: US supreme court rules Trump’s firing of Lisa Cook from Fed was unconstitutional
items.standfirst: Justices find president does not have constitutional authority to fire Federal Reserve governor without causeUS supreme court decisions – live updatesSign up for the Breaking News US emailThe US supreme court has ruled that Donald Trump’s firing of a Federal Reserve governor was unconstitutional, in a landmark ruling that limits a president’s authority over the central bank.In its opinion, the court said that Trump does not have the constitutional authority to fire a Fed governor without cause. Continue reading...
items.content: Justices find president does not have constitutional authority to fire Federal Reserve governor without causeUS supreme court decisions – live updatesSign up for the Breaking News US emailThe US supreme court has ruled that Donald Trump’s firing of a Federal Reserve governor was unconstitutional, in a landmark ruling that limits a president’s authority over the central bank.In its opinion, the court said that Trump does not have the constitutional authority to fire a Fed governor without cause. Continue reading...

items.title: US supreme court upholds law to count mail-in ballots arriving after election day
items.standfirst: Court sides against Republicans after deciding earlier this term to let Louisiana effectively dismantle Voting Rights ActUS supreme court decisions – live updatesSign up for the Breaking News US newsletter emailThe US supreme court sided against national Republicans and Donald Trump’s administration to allow mail-in ballots that arrive after election day to be counted, upholding the law in more than a dozen states.The Republican National Committee (RNC) had challenged a Mississippi state law allowing mailed ballots to be counted if they arrive within five business days of election day, so long as they were postmarked by election day. Continue reading...
items.content: Court sides against Republicans after deciding earlier this term to let Louisiana effectively dismantle Voting Rights ActUS supreme court decisions – live updatesSign up for the Breaking News US newsletter emailThe US supreme court sided against national Republicans and Donald Trump’s administration to allow mail-in ballots that arrive after election day to be counted, upholding the law in more than a dozen states.The Republican National Committee (RNC) had challenged a Mississippi state law allowing mailed ballots to be counted if they arrive within five business days of election day, so long as they were postmarked by election day. Continue reading...

items.title: The Supreme Court upholds grace periods for mail-in ballots, siding against the GOP
items.standfirst: The U.S. Supreme Court has upheld a Mississippi law that allows election officials to count mail-in ballots that are postmarked by Election Day but received up to five days after it.
items.content: The U.S. Supreme Court has upheld a Mississippi law that allows election officials to count mail-in ballots that are postmarked by Election Day but received up to five days after it.(Image credit: Rebecca Droke)

items.standfirst: President Trump said Monday on social media that the U.S. and Iran will meet in Doha, Qatar, on Tuesday despite trading strikes over the weekend. Iran has not confirmed the apparent talks. CBS News' Olivia Rinaldi and Aaron MacLean have more.

clusters.title: Comcast plans to split into 2 companies, spinning off NBCUniversal
clusters.neutral_headline: Comcast to spin off NBCUniversal and Sky into separate media business
bullet_summary: Current shareholders would receive shares in both companies under the planned split, Comcast said Monday
The move comes eight years after the US group, which said the separation will take a year to complete, acquired Sky’s European operations for £31bn
Comcast said it plans to spin off NBCUniversal and Sky into a new publicly traded company as it looks to separate its media businesses from its cable-TV and internet operations

bullet_summary: Reported by France24, NPR News, Guardian World and others

items.title: Afghanistan: at least 36 killed in deadly Pakistani strikes
items.standfirst: At least 36 people were killed across three provinces of eastern #Afghanistan in Pakistani strikes. #Islamabad says it was targeting a group responsible for an attack the day before.
items.content: At least 36 people were killed across three provinces of eastern #Afghanistan in Pakistani strikes. #Islamabad says it was targeting a group responsible for an attack the day before.

items.title: Pakistani airstrikes kill 36 civilians in Afghanistan and wound 160, officials say
items.standfirst: Pakistan says it targeted militant hideouts along the Pakistan-Afghanistan border in response to militant attacks across Pakistan. Afghanistan condemned the strikes as acts of aggression.
items.content: Pakistan says it targeted militant hideouts along the Pakistan-Afghanistan border in response to militant attacks across Pakistan. Afghanistan condemned the strikes as acts of aggression. (Image credit: Saifullah Zahir)

items.title: Pakistani airstrikes kill dozens in eastern Afghanistan
items.standfirst: Pakistan says strikes were aimed at a terrorist group while Taliban condemn ‘cowardly act of aggression’Pakistani airstrikes in three eastern provinces of Afghanistan killed 36 civilians and wounded 163 others, Afghan officials have said, as attacks between the two countries showed no sign of abating.Pakistan’s information minister, Attaullah Tarar, said the operations on Sunday night were aimed at a terrorist group his country blamed for a deadly militant attack in Karachi that killed three security personnel over the weekend. Continue reading...
items.content: Pakistan says strikes were aimed at a terrorist group while Taliban condemn ‘cowardly act of aggression’Pakistani airstrikes in three eastern provinces of Afghanistan killed 36 civilians and wounded 163 others, Afghan officials have said, as attacks between the two countries showed no sign of abating.Pakistan’s information minister, Attaullah Tarar, said the operations on Sunday night were aimed at a terrorist group his country blamed for a deadly militant attack in Karachi that killed three security personnel over the weekend. Continue reading...

items.title: Pakistan strikes eastern Afghanistan as Taliban says civilians killed
items.standfirst: Pakistan's security forces killed at least 29 militants in ground ​and air operations along the Afghanistan border, it said on Monday, while the Afghan Taliban said at least 38 civilians were killed in airstrikes. France 24's correspodent in Islamabad, Shazaib Wahlah, as the latest.
items.content: Pakistan's security forces killed at least 29 militants in ground ​and air operations along the Afghanistan border, it said on Monday, while the Afghan Taliban said at least 38 civilians were killed in airstrikes. France 24's correspodent in Islamabad, Shazaib Wahlah, as the latest.

items.title: Pakistani strikes kill dozens in Afghanistan

items.content: The Taliban government says civilians were killed, while Pakistan maintains they targeted militants.

clusters.title: Putin admits fuel shortages after Ukrainian attacks
clusters.neutral_headline: Putin admits Ukrainian drone strikes are driving Russian fuel shortages
bullet_summary: “Of course, they create problems, that’s obvious,” Putin said
Reported by NBC World, France24, Guardian World

items.title: Putin admits fuel shortages after Ukrainian attacks
items.standfirst: Russian President Vladimir Putin acknowledged for the first time Sunday that Russia is facing fuel shortages following a wave of Ukrainian attacks deep inside the country.
items.content: Russian President Vladimir Putin acknowledged for the first time Sunday that Russia is facing fuel shortages following a wave of Ukrainian attacks deep inside the country.

items.title: Ukraine strikes another Russian oil refinery as Putin admits fuel shortages
items.standfirst: Ukraine kept up its heavy drone assault on Russia, setting fire to a major oil refinery in the south, as President Vladimir Putin acknowledged for the first time on Sunday that the country was facing a “certain deficit” of fuel and vowed to strengthen protection of oil facilities and boost fuel output.
items.content: Ukraine kept up its heavy drone assault on Russia, setting fire to a major oil refinery in the south, as President Vladimir Putin acknowledged for the first time on Sunday that the country was facing a “certain deficit” of fuel and vowed to strengthen protection of oil facilities and boost fuel output.

items.title: Putin admits Ukrainian drone strikes are driving Russian fuel shortages
items.standfirst: President says attacks on infrastructure are causing ‘obvious’ problems but that they are ‘not critical’Vladimir Putin has admitted Russia is facing fuel shortages as Ukraine steps up its long-range drone campaign, with repeated strikes setting oil refineries ablaze and forcing multiple regions to introduce unprecedented petrol rationing.Speaking to Russian state television late on Sunday, the Russian president acknowledged for the first time that Ukrainian attacks on energy infrastructure were affecting domestic fuel supplies. “Of course, they create problems, that’s obvious,” Putin said. “Right now we’re observing a certain shortage, but it’s not critical.” Continue reading...
items.content: President says attacks on infrastructure are causing ‘obvious’ problems but that they are ‘not critical’Vladimir Putin has admitted Russia is facing fuel shortages as Ukraine steps up its long-range drone campaign, with repeated strikes setting oil refineries ablaze and forcing multiple regions to introduce unprecedented petrol rationing.Speaking to Russian state television late on Sunday, the Russian president acknowledged for the first time that Ukrainian attacks on energy infrastructure were affecting domestic fuel supplies. “Of course, they create problems, that’s obvious,” Putin said. “Right now we’re observing a certain shortage, but it’s not critical.” Continue reading...

clusters.neutral_headline: Supreme Court cements Trump's power over agencies long considered independent

items.title: Supreme Court cements Trump's power over agencies long considered independent
items.standfirst: In a 6-3 decision, the Supreme Court struck down a 91-year-old precedent that has prevented presidents from removing members of independent agencies meant to be a check on his power.
items.content: In a 6-3 decision, the Supreme Court struck down a 91-year-old precedent that has prevented presidents from removing members of independent agencies meant to be a check on his power.(Image credit: Kevin Dietsch)

items.title: Supreme Court strikes down limits on Trump removing FTC members
items.standfirst: The Supreme Court overturned a 90-year-old decision that allowed Congress to shield members of certain independent agencies from being fired by the president at will.
items.content: The Supreme Court overturned a 90-year-old decision that allowed Congress to shield members of certain independent agencies from being fired by the president at will.

items.title: British American Tobacco to slash 9,000 jobs as it turns to AI
items.standfirst: Dunhill maker to cut about a fifth of workforce, aiming to reduce costs and become more ‘technology enabled’Business live – latest updatesBritish American Tobacco (BAT) will cut about a fifth of its 47,000-strong workforce this year, as the cigarette-maker looks for ways to push down costs and become more “technology enabled”.BAT, which is one of the biggest tobacco groups in the world, has announced it will cut 5,500 jobs by the end of the year and outsource a further 3,500, affecting a ⁠total of 9,000 employees. clusters.neutral_headline: Eleven killed after plane carrying skydivers crashes in eastern France
bullet_summary: The pilot and 10 passengers - including five first-time parachutists - died in the incident, local officials said
Eleven people were killed when ⁠a small plane crashed in the northeastern French town of Tomblaine, the local prefecture ⁠said
